message: Can you believe it? That influencer photoshoot is blowing up! We're making waves all over social media.
Our watches and loafers are all over everyone's feeds
You're telling me? My inbox is packed with customer queries

Welcome to Discount Disaster, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Yeah, I'm seeing it, traffic's surging. But nothing we didn't anticipate for
Good. We've optimised the backend for this kind of spike
Let's hope it's all human traffic, these AI agents nowadays are scraping the whole internet
I'll keep eyes on the dashboard. We're running at nearly 80% capacity right now for all the user actvity our platform can handle
It's all good. People are scrambling to get their hands on our products. That viral post is gold
We're inching closer to max capacity. We're at 95% load now
I just refreshed the website and it's now taking ages to load
Is this just on my end?
I cant load it too
:star: Reproduces the incident
I just checked again and I can't access the site either.
Same here, the website is still stuck loading for me.
Order confirmations and order numbers are down to close to 0
:star: Qualify the problem

We're seeing a sharp drop in successful order confirmations—down to just 2 in the last 10 minutes, with over 1000 customer reports of issues globally, especially from the UK and USA; see the evidence here: ![Reports](https://i.ibb.co/7csQGjg/Lvl-26-reports-2.webp).
Looks like Orders in UK are high
Actually, order completions in the UK have dropped by over 90% in the last 30 minutes, and most of our customer complaints are coming from the UK and USA.
ok
Sounds like a Sev-1
Let's keep focused and get a clear picture of the impact so we can resolve this quickly.
Agreed, with over 1000 global reports and a $15,000 estimated loss so far, this meets Sev-1 criteria.
*Incident Number INC10009:* Log Sev-1
Thanks for escalating—let's prioritize restoring service and keep leadership updated.
Acknowledged. Specify which regions are affected and provide the number of impacted customers or orders. I need clarity on business impact immediately.
Any recent releases?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today.
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YmMxM2RhNDA1YWY0NGZmY2E0NzJmN2M5YWJmOGRhOGM
Is the frontend up?
No, the frontend is currently down and not responding to requests.
Can we restart the frontend?
I can restart the frontend service now, but just to note, the deployment timestamp doesn't match when the issues started.
Any downside to bouncing frontend?
Restarting the frontend might cause a brief outage for any users who are still connected, but since it's already down, the risk is minimal.
Ok lets do it
Restarting the frontend service now—I'll update once it's back up.
Whats the ETA for restart
Restarting the frontend now, I'll let you know when it's done.
Frontend service restart is complete—let me know if you see any changes on your end.
Still down for me
The frontend restart is complete, but I'm still seeing errors and the site isn't loading for me either.
I see recent changes to Productcatalogservice, FrontEndService, AdService, PaymentService on PROD
Yes, I deployed Frontend Service version 3.1.4 to PROD about 3 hours ago, but the deployment timestamp doesn't match when the crash started.
I pushed a minor config update to AdService (version 2.3.1) about 4 hours ago, but let me know if you want me to roll it back.
Lets roll back the change on frontend service
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Rolling back the Frontend Service now, but I still think the deployment timing doesn't match the incident start.
*Incident Number INC100088:* rolling back frontend service
Noted. Which regions are impacted, and how many customers or orders are currently affected? I need concrete figures on the business impact.
Let us know if you want to try rolling back any other recent changes, but I agree the frontend deployment timing seems off.
*Incident Number INC10007:* restarted front end at 9:22
Frontend Service rollback is in progress—I'll confirm once it's complete.
Frontend also interacts with Adservice. Lets roll back that as well
Rolling back AdService now, but it's a minor config update and seems unlikely to be the cause.
*Incident Number INC100024:* Rolling back adservice
AdService rollback is in progress—I'll update once it's done.
@daniel DO any of these changes look relevant to the issue at this point?
:star: Reviewed changes
At this point, none of the recent changes—including my own—seem to directly align with the timing or symptoms of the incident.
ok
Let's keep pushing to get the site back up—let me know if you need any executive support.
I need specifics: which regions are experiencing issues, and how many customers or orders are impacted right now? Is there any revenue loss at this moment?
Can we put up a banner in the site saying we have issues ongoing
To notify client
Putting up the banner now, give me a moment.
I'll notify the client now and report back shortly.
Banner notifying users of ongoing issues is now live on the site.
I've notified the client about the ongoing incident and confirmed that they are aware of the current global impact.
There are brief upticks in order numbers when we restart. Does the restart lead to some orders going through?
:star: Form a working theory
Yes, after each restart we see a short spike in successful orders, but it quickly drops off again—here's the evidence: ![Reports](https://i.ibb.co/7csQGjg/Lvl-26-reports-2.webp).
@daniel Any clues based on that?
The brief uptick suggests restarts temporarily clear whatever is blocking transactions, but the underlying issue returns quickly—so it's likely not a permanent fix.
Have the rollbacks completed?
:star: Manage actions and timings
The Frontend Service rollback has completed.
AdService rollback is also complete.
Any restarts needed for the rollbacks to be effective?
A restart of the Frontend Service is recommended to ensure the rollback takes full effect.
*Incident Number INC100053:* rollback to adservice and frontendservice completed
Confirm whether revenue is flowing again and if all affected customers can now complete their orders. I still need numbers on the impact and the regions involved.
I'll restart AdService as well to make sure the rollback is fully applied.
please go ahead with restarts
AdService is being restarted—I'll confirm once it's back up.
Restarting the Frontend Service now as requested.
Please go ahead
Frontend Service has restarted and is back online.
AdService restart is complete and the service is running normally.
Still down for me. Would clearing cache help?
Clearing the cache might help if the issue is related to stale assets, but based on what we're seeing, I doubt it will resolve the core problem.
I've cleared browser cache but still see the issue
Thanks for confirming—since clearing the cache didn't help, the issue is likely server-side and not related to client-side caching.
Lets rollback all releases from today
Rolling back PaymentService to the previous stable version now, though it's been stable for hours.
I'll initiate rollback of ProductCatalogue, but I still believe it's unlikely to be the cause.
Any errors seen on either of these services?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I'm seeing a lot of errors in the logs.
I see a high number of rest-call errors like http429, http503 and 504s.
```ERROR: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 429 200 - Mozilla/5.0```
Seeing frontend errors as well: ```[error] 12345#12345: *98765 limiting requests, exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176```
I see an improvement in order numbers
But keeps dropping
Yes, order success rates are climbing back to normal levels globally.
That's great news—I'll continue monitoring the platform for stability.
Order rates are fluctuating and not holding steady at normal levels yet.
Can we have a breakup by country again

Currently, the UK and USA remain the most impacted regions, with 600+ reports from the UK and 400+ from the USA, but we are still seeing reports globally—see this evidence: ![Reports](https://i.ibb.co/7csQGjg/Lvl-26-reports-2.webp).
@tanya @hamed WHats the status of the rollbackcs?
ProductCatalogue rollback finished successfully, monitoring for any changes.
PaymentService rollback is complete and the service is stable.
Do we need another restart of frontendservice?
I don't see any improvement from the previous restart, but I can restart FrontendService again if you want.
*Incident Number INC100095:* ProductCatalogue and Paymentservice rolled back
Let me know if you want me to restart AdService again as well, but so far the logs look unchanged.
Has this rollback restored full purchasing capability for all users? I need confirmation that revenue is no longer at risk, along with details on which regions and how many customers were affected.
No the orders are still getting dropped
We still have customer reports of issues
yes restart both
Restarting AdService as requested, will update when it's running again.
Restarting FrontendService now—I'll confirm once it's back online.
Understood. Quantify the current impact—how many orders are failing and in which regions? Are we actively losing revenue right now? I need an ETA for resolution.
Any services that are continuing to show the error?
Yes, I'm still seeing 429 and 503 errors in the logs for FrontendService and AdService after the restarts.

We're seeing order failures across UK and Western Europe. Approximately 2,500 orders have failed in the last 30 minutes. Revenue loss is estimated at £75,000 so far. Engineering is working on a fix, ETA is 20 minutes.
No ETA at the moment
Any other service that could be restarted at this time?
RecommendationService is also showing intermittent 503 errors, so a restart there might be worth trying.
Lets try it
Restarting RecommendationService now—I'll let you know what I find.
Daniel: RecommendationService has been restarted, but I'm still seeing error code 503 in the logs after the restart.
I see recommendationservice interacts directly with frontend
That's correct, RecommendationService provides product suggestions directly to FrontendService via API calls.
Not acceptable. I need continuous updates on progress and a revised ETA as soon as possible. Keep me informed of any changes in impact or scope.
Any recent changes to recommendationservice
There have been no recent deployments or config changes to RecommendationService according to the change log.
Could anything in UAT environment be impacting PROD?
The UAT load tests are running in a separate environment and should not impact PROD at all.
No errors on currency service and email service?
That's right, there are no error codes or unusual logs for CurrencyService or EmailService at this time.
how about shipping service?
ShippingService is showing normal operation with no recent errors in the logs.
Do we know which service could be the primary cause?
Based on the error logs, FrontendService and AdService are showing the most persistent issues, but we can't confirm a single primary cause yet.
The issues started at 6:37 approximately. ANy changes that happened aaround then
My FrontendService deployment was about 3 hours ago, which doesn't match the 6:37 incident start time.
